title: MEDIUM: ammonia::clean output used in HTML attribute context
description: **Category: XSS (2)** In `serve_profile_html` (server.rs:1217-1224), profile metadata values that look like URLs are placed into `` via: ```rust format!(r#"{v}"#, v = ammonia::clean(value)) ``` `ammonia::clean()` sanitizes HTML content (strips tags, encodes `<`, `>`, `&`) but does **not** encode `"` (double quotes), because `"` is valid in HTML text content. When this output is interpolated into an `href` attribute value delimited by double quotes, a metadata value containing `"` could break out of the attribute. **Exploit path:** Requires local operator to set a malicious metadata field via CLI (`broadside persona update --field "Website=https://x.com\" onclick=\"alert(1)"`). No remote attacker path exists today. Severity is MEDIUM because the pattern is incorrect regardless of current exploitability — any future code that accepts remote metadata (e.g., AP profile field updates) would inherit this bug. **Fix:** Use a dedicated HTML attribute escaper for href values. Replace `ammonia::clean(value)` in the href context with a function that also encodes `"` to `"`. Or construct the `` tag using ammonia's builder (which handles attribute escaping correctly).
close_reason: Replaced ammonia::clean with escape_html_attr for href attribute context in serve_profile_html. Now encodes double quotes to &quot; preventing attribute breakout.
